channel.title: SevenAtSevenPolitics
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenPolitics, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: ‘Presidents Day’ Is George Washington Erasure. America 250 Is The Perfect Time To Fix It
item.description: Contrary to popular belief, the federal holiday celebrated this Feb. 16 is not 'President’s Day' — the true name of the holiday, per federal law is 'W...

item.title: America borrowed $43.5 billion a week in the first four months of the fiscal year, with debt interest on track to be over $1 trillion for 2026
item.description: "The longer lawmakers wait, the higher the price for Americans," warned Maya MacGuineas, president of the Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget.

item.title: Trump administration to make it harder for federal workers to appeal firing
item.description: The Trump administration on Monday proposed a new rule that would make it harder for fired federal employees to get their jobs back by limiting their ...

item.title: Trump admin to repeal Obama-era greenhouse gas finding in large-scale deregulation
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration is set to reverse a 2009 greenhouse gas finding in what EPA chief calls "largest deregulation" in U.S. history...

item.title: New York Times Does The Impossible And Interviews A Reasonable American On Immigration
item.description: 'They talk about the business owners in this country being the backbone of the country. But politicians don’t believe that, or they don’t act on that....

item.title: House finds rare moment of unity to tackle housing affordability
item.description: Legislation to address housing affordability passed Monday with overwhelming support in the House, where lawmakers on both sides of the aisle have hom...

item.title: Bretton AI raises $75 million to use AI to combat financial crime
item.description: With new funding from Sapphire Ventures and Greylock Partners, the startup is upending anti-money laundering prevention at top financial firms.

item.title: Why that $2 trillion software stock wipeout didn’t derail the AI bull market
item.description: 75% of companies in the S&P 500 have reported Q4 earnings, and profit per share across those companies is 12% higher than it was a year ago.

item.title: Trump threatens to block Canadian-U.S. bridge as major trading partner turns toward China
item.description: President Trump on Monday threatened to block the opening of a bridge between Michigan and Ontario until the U.S. is given control of half of it as we...

item.title: The civil war inside American Airlines may not be all CEO Robert Isom’s fault
item.description: Also: All the news and watercooler chat from Fortune.

item.title: China’s Monstrous Sentencing of Jimmy Lai
item.description: Foreign nations should think twice about flirting with Beijing as a reliable partner.

item.title: The UAE Quietly Poured a Half-Billion Dollars into Trump’s Crypto Venture Four Days Before Inauguration
item.description: Timing is everything.

item.title: Iran draws missile red line as analysts warn Tehran is stalling US talks
item.description: Iran rejects U.S. demands to negotiate ballistic missile program as tensions escalate across Middle East despite ongoing diplomatic talks in Oman.

item.title: Why Canada Engaged China Ahead of the USMCA Review
item.description: The country’s trade deal with Beijing is risk-management under pressure, not a pivot away from the United States.

item.title: This CEO wants to do for hearing aids what she helped do for shapewear at Spanx
item.description: Laurie Ann Goldman, who helped turn Spanx into a global powerhouse, is now CEO of Audien Hearing.

item.title: House passes bills to create 250th anniversary commemorative coins, congressional time capsule
item.description: The House on Monday passed a pair of bills in honor of America's 250th anniversary that would create a commemorative coin and a congressional time cap...

item.title: Senate races to avert third shutdown as DHS deal takes shape
item.description: Department of Homeland Security faces a potential shutdown affecting TSA, FEMA and the Coast Guard, as Senate Democrats push for immigration enforceme...

item.title: As Ukraine war drags on, Trump hits Putin by squeezing Russia’s proxies
item.description: President Donald Trump threatened "very severe consequences" on Russia in 2025 if Moscow didn't end Ukraine war, pressuring Russian allies Iran and Ve...

item.title: Adam Schiff makes endorsement in California gubernatorial race
item.description: Democratic Sen. Adam Schiff of California is backing his House Democratic colleague Rep. Eric Swalwell in the Golden State governor's race.

item.title: The Trump Administration Defeated The ‘Seditious Six’ By ‘Going On Offense’
item.description: The 'Seditious Six' called for a mutiny, and the administration decided to 'go on offense and make them tap-dance a little.'

item.title: Seattle Won the Super Bowl, but Its Economy Has Gone from Boom to Gloom
item.description: It’s time for Washington State’s voters to wake up to the dangers that progressive lawmakers pose.

item.title: Pro-China Forces Sentence Hong Kong Freedom Fighter Jimmy Lai To 20 Years In Prison
item.description: In a further erosion of freedom in Hong Kong, the city’s Chinese-backed government sentenced media mogul and pro-democracy activist Jimmy Lai to 20 ye...

item.title: White House sends counteroffer to Democrats' immigration enforcement demands
item.description: The White House on Monday sent a counteroffer to Democrats' 10-point list of immigration enforcement demands, as the two parties negotiate a Departmen...

item.title: Tony Hinchcliffe Was Right
item.description: Despite Bad Bunny's arrogance, Puerto Rico would probably more resemble a hellscape than anything else without the patronage of the American people.

item.title: Is Motiveless Political Violence Really on the Rise?
item.description: We must be able to call domestic political terrorism by its proper name.

item.title: Rethinking Household Asset Allocation Under Capital Constraints
item.description: Leveraged ETFs aren’t about more risk, they’re about using less capital to achieve a desired risk profile in household portfolios. The post Rethinking...

item.title: Stockholm’s Capital Markets Success: More Than Meatballs
item.description: Stockholm’s capital-market success reflects more than IPOs and PE activity. It offers a case study in how culture, institutions, and incentives shape ...

item.title: Where AI Ends and Investment Judgment Begins
item.description: AI’s growing capabilities challenge traditional investment skill, shifting competitive advantage toward governance, process, and judgment. The post Wh...

item.title: Three Risks of Relying on the S&P 500 in Retirement Planning
item.description: Why S&P 500 reliance can undermine retirement outcomes, and how diversification, valuation discipline, and withdrawals reshape long-term portfolio ris...

item.title: Book Review: Principles of Bitcoin
item.description: A rigorous exploration of bitcoin reveals a new framework for understanding money beyond traditional financial models. The post Book Review: Principle...
